title: Former Middleborough fire captain charged for allegedly secretly filming nude stepdaughter
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Eric Gunnison, 59, former Middleborough fire captain, charged with secretly filming stepdaughter using hidden cameras in bathroom.
Gunnison faces 23 counts of illegal surveillance, unauthorized computer access, and harassment; pleaded not guilty in Wareham District Court.
Police investigation found 150 images on Gunnison's iPad; hidden cameras not recovered but Gunnison allegedly claimed to have destroyed them.

title: How to watch the Parade of Sail by boat
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The Parade of Sail on July 11 will feature over 60 tall ships from more than 20 countries in Boston Harbor.
Various charters and cruises, such as Hindu Charters and Tall Ships Boston Cruise, offer Parade of Sail viewing experiences.
Classic Sail Boston provides a private charter aboard Captiva for the Parade of Sail, with other festival charters available.

title: Officials identify cyclist, 15, killed in Franklin crash as Bishop Feehan student
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Oliver Lewis, 15, a Bishop Feehan High School student, died after being hit by a vehicle in Franklin.
The incident occurred near King Street and I-495; the driver stayed at the scene and is cooperating with police.
Counseling services are available at Bishop Feehan High School; details of funeral services are not yet announced.

title: NBA Insider on Jaylen Brown: ‘He’s going to get traded’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: NBA insider Brian Windhorst expects the Boston Celtics to trade Jaylen Brown following failed trade efforts for Giannis Antetokounmpo.
Celtics GM Brad Stevens suggested a potential trade to Brown, highlighting active trade talks and a noncommittal stance on Brown's future.
Jaylen Brown, a five-time All-Star, played a key role this season, but remains under trade consideration despite a contract through 2029.

title: Review & setlists: David Lee Roth and Sammy Hagar both hit Greater Boston. Who rocked harder?
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: David Lee Roth performed at House of Blues, Boston, on May 21, offering a Van Halen-heavy 18-song set.
Sammy Hagar played June 24 at Xfinity Center, Mansfield, showcasing a 19-song set including 11 Van Halen tracks.
Concert review favored Sammy Hagar's performance, praising his energy and setlist, supported by guitarist Joe Satriani.

title: How did it get this bad for the Red Sox, and where should they go from here?
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox face struggles with a 32-46 record, currently last in MLB after losses to the Rockies.
Injuries and underperformance of key players like Roman Anthony and Trevor Story have impacted the team.
Critics highlight the need for veteran players and better player development to improve future seasons.

title: Boston has one of the best free attractions in America, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: USA Today ranked Boston's Freedom Trail as the 7th best free attraction in America in its 2026 10Best Readers’ Choice Awards.
The Freedom Trail is a 2.5-mile path connecting 16 historical sites, attracting over 4 million visitors annually.
USA Today's top free attraction is the Saint Louis Zoo; the Freedom Trail highlights sites like Faneuil Hall and the USS Constitution.

title: ‘Until next time Boston’: Scottish newspaper ad thanks city for welcoming Tartan Army
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The Tartan Army thanked Boston with a full-page ad in Scotland's The Herald newspaper after their World Cup visit.
During their stay, Scottish fans attended Red Sox games, donated to charities, and interacted warmly with locals.
Boston and Glasgow may become sister cities following the World Cup, as proposed by Mayor Michelle Wu.

title: A 3-bedroom 1800s Colonial in Marblehead — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: The Marblehead Colonial was built in 1842 and offers 2,136 square feet of living space.
This New England style home features 3 bedrooms, 2 full bathrooms, and one half bathroom.
The annual property taxes for this home are $11,359 in 2026.

title: After a tense process, City Council accepts Wu’s amended budget. Here’s what to know.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council accepted Mayor Michelle Wu's $4.9 billion amended budget, ending a tense budget season.
Wu accepted nearly all council amendments, avoiding layoffs by reallocating funds within the Transportation Department.
Protests and arrests highlighted council meetings as tensions persisted over proposed budget cuts and funding priorities.
